the smell of wet concrete and cold coffee
the faint hum of a ceiling fluorescent
the creak of floorboards under her weight
the sharp tang of diesel in the morning air
the muffled bass of a car stereo from a passing van
the drip of a tap in an unseen kitchen
a radio murmuring in another room
the scent of damp wool drying on a radiator
the rustle of a newspaper being folded
a kettle clicking off in the next room
the taste of iron on the back of the tongue
the soft percussion of rain on a corrugated roof
the faint residue of cigarette smoke on fabric
the squeak of rubber soles on polished lino
the low whistle of wind through a cracked window frame
the warmth of a radiator against a bare calf
the dry rasp of paper being torn
the bitter edge of over-brewed tea
the whine of a distant alarm
the waxy smell of an extinguished candle
the flat metallic scent of winter air
the cloying sweetness of an old air freshener
the hiss of a gas ring on low
the click of a light switch flipped twice
the murmur of a television in an empty living room
the grit of plaster dust between the teeth
the catch in her throat before she spoke
the stubborn chill of an unheated hallway
the faint stink of drains after heavy rain
the brittle crunch of gravel underfoot
the smell of school corridors at lunchtime
the cold curve of a metal banister
the resinous scent of pine needles crushed on a step
the slap of wet trainers on a tiled floor
the dry texture of bedsheets slept in too long
the faint chemical tang of bleach
the mechanical sigh of a boiler kicking in
the soft friction of denim on a wooden bench
the taste of old bread left out overnight
the sting of a draught from a bad window seal
the low thrum of a fridge against the kitchen wall
the papery rattle of leaves at the base of a fence
the close smell of a cheap hotel pillow
the wet gleam of a tarmac lane after midnight
the blood-warm smell of overworked electrics
the grainy taste of instant coffee
the insistent tick of a cheap plastic clock
the rasp of a zip being pulled slowly
the stale heat of an upstairs room in August
the flat metallic clang of a bin lid dropped
the dry whisper of net curtains in a draught
the faint sweetness of pipe tobacco on a coat
the gritty crunch of sugar on a wet counter
the scrape of a chair being pushed back
the burnt-plastic smell of a long-on toaster
the soft drag of a broom across a concrete yard
the salt edge of sea air three streets inland
the oily residue of engine rags on a workbench
the ringing stillness after a slammed door
the sour warmth of unwashed laundry
the cracked rubber smell of old car seats
the echo of footsteps in a municipal stairwell
the cold lick of tile against bare feet
the hum of a strip light five metres away
the unmistakable tang of a public swimming pool
the faint vanilla of old paperbacks
the tickle of a loose thread against her wrist
the harshness of porch gravel at three in the morning
the sticky residue of spilled lemonade on a windowsill
the thick silence of snow on a neighbourhood
the oily slick of a recently cleaned floor
the close weight of a badly ventilated bathroom
the sharp clean of a pine-scented disinfectant
the raw edge of a splinter in a wooden rail
the smell of frost on a parked car bonnet
the warm dust of an attic left closed for a year
the scratch of wool against a neck in summer
the hot acrid burn of an iron left face-down
the whisper of a page being turned alone in a room
the stale breath of a radiator that hasn't been bled
the peculiar quiet of a pub before opening
the faint chemical lemon of a staff toilet
the sticky tape residue on a fridge door
the heavy sweetness of funeral flowers
the toasted smell of a coat left near a heater
the bitter jolt of coffee drunk too fast
the yielding softness of an old leather chair
the bruised smell of wet ferns by a wall
the hum of wires above a railway line
the gritty pavement under a pair of tired shoes
the slick of condensation on a cold bus window
the tight press of damp clothes on skin
the distant drone of a single-prop aircraft
the soft collapse of a bag of groceries onto a counter
the dry whisper of a match being struck
the warmth of a mug held in both hands
the mineral taste of tap water from an old pipe
the nervous tick of a single blinking bulb
the dust in a slant of afternoon light
the faint rattle of an unsecured window
the tacky feel of a badly painted banister
the sulphur pop of a gas hob catching
